skill.name: ai-product
skill.description: Every product will be AI-powered. The question is whether you'll build it right or ship a demo that falls apart in production. This skill covers LLM integration patterns, RAG architecture, prompt engineering, and production-ready AI product development.
skill.summary: Ship production-ready AI products with LLM integration patterns, RAG architecture, and validated outputs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 2 false positive patterns related to 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3 and 58. These are misidentified keywords ('patterns', 'architecture') in documentation text. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code, network calls, scripts, filesystem access, or external commands. Safe for publication.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Misidentified Cryptographic Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner detected 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:58 - This is generic usage text with no cryptographic content. False positive from keyword detection.

content.value_statement: Transform AI demos into production systems. This skill teaches LLM integration patterns, RAG architecture, and validation strategies so you can ship AI features that work at scale.
actual_capabilities: Apply structured output patterns with function calling and JSON schema validation
Implement streaming responses to reduce perceived latency in AI applications
Design prompt versioning and testing strategies for production systems
Identify and avoid common AI demo-ware pitfalls
Optimize context window usage to reduce costs and improve performance
Build validation layers to catch harmful outputs and hallucinations
limitations: This skill provides guidance but does not execute code or make API calls
Does not include pre-built code templates or implementation libraries
Does not provide specific model recommendations or API configuration
Requires additional research for compliance with specific regulations

use_cases.title: Architecting LLM Applications
use_cases.description: Design production systems that use LLMs safely and reliably with proper validation and error handling.
use_cases.target_user: Software architects and senior developers building AI-powered products

use_cases.title: Validating AI Outputs
use_cases.description: Implement safety systems and validation layers to catch hallucinations and harmful content before serving to users.
use_cases.target_user: Engineers responsible for AI safety and content moderation

use_cases.title: Optimizing AI Costs
use_cases.description: Reduce LLM API costs by 80% through prompt optimization, context management, and efficient token usage.
use_cases.target_user: Product teams optimizing AI feature costs and performance

prompt_templates.title: Design AI Product Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to build an AI-powered feature that [describe use case]. Help me design a production-ready architecture including: 1) How to structure prompts for reliability 2) What validation layers I need 3) How to handle failures gracefully 4) Cost optimization strategies
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new AI feature development project

prompt_templates.title: Review Prompt for Production
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this prompt for production deployment: [insert prompt]. Identify: 1) Potential failure modes 2) Missing validation steps 3) Context window optimization opportunities 4) Cost concerns
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing a prompt for production use

prompt_templates.title: Fix Hallucination Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: My AI system is producing hallucinations in [specific context]. The current prompt is [insert prompt]. Suggest modifications to reduce hallucinations while maintaining accuracy.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Structured Output
prompt_templates.prompt: I need my LLM to return [describe desired output format]. Help me: 1) Design the schema 2) Write the prompt with proper instructions 3) Add validation logic 4) Handle parsing failures gracefully

output_examples.input: Help me design a customer support AI that validates all outputs before responding to users
output_examples.output:
Here's a production-ready architecture for your customer support AI:

**Prompt Structure**: System prompt with strict output format instructions, user query in template with validation hooks

**Validation Layers**:
1. Schema validation - ensure response matches expected JSON structure
2. Safety check - scan for harmful content before responding
3. Factual verification - cross-reference claims with knowledge base
4. Format enforcement - validate response length and structure

**Error Handling**: Graceful degradation with fallback to human agent when confidence is low

output_examples.input: My LLM is too slow. How can I improve perceived latency?
output_examples.output:
Implement streaming with these techniques:

1. **Server-Sent Events (SSE)**: Stream tokens as they're generated
2. **Progressive UI**: Show skeleton first, fill in content as it arrives
3. **Optimistic Updates**: Display likely response while AI generates
4. **Chunked Generation**: Break complex responses into smaller pieces

This can reduce perceived latency by 40-60% without faster model response times.

best_practices: Treat prompts as code: version control, test in CI/CD, and review changes through pull requests
Always validate LLM outputs with schema validation and safety checks before using them
Build defense in depth: multiple validation layers catch what single checks miss
anti_patterns: Shipping AI demos without production hardening - users will encounter failures at scale
Stuffing context windows with irrelevant data - increases costs and reduces accuracy
Trusting LLM outputs without validation - hallucinations will reach production users

faq.question: Does this skill include code templates?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides architectural guidance and best practices. You will need to implement the patterns in your own codebase.

faq.question: Which LLM providers does this skill support?
faq.answer: The patterns are provider-agnostic and apply to any LLM including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and open-source models.

faq.question: How do I test prompts in production?
faq.answer: Version prompts in your codebase, create regression test suites with known inputs and expected outputs, and monitor for degradation over time.

faq.question: What validation should I implement?
faq.answer: At minimum: schema validation (ensure correct format), safety checks (harmful content), and factual verification (for claims that need to be accurate).

faq.question: How can I reduce LLM costs?
faq.answer: Optimize prompts to use fewer tokens, implement caching for repeated queries, use smaller models for simple tasks, and monitor per-request costs.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: This skill is best for developers with programming experience. Beginners should first understand basic prompt engineering before applying production patterns.
